title: rhododendron ponticum subsp. baeticum
intro: Rhododendron ponticum subsp. baeticum, also known as the Baetic rhododendron, is a subspecies of the Rhododendron ponticum plant, native to the Iberian Peninsula in southwestern Europe. This shrub has been used in traditional medicine for centuries, valued for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant's leaves and flowers have been used to treat various health issues, such as fever, sore throats, and respiratory problems, offering potential health benefits like reduced inflammation and improved immune function. Herbal preparations of Rhododendron ponticum subsp. baeticum include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various ailments and support overall well-being.
